questions.question: A recipe for making cookies includes $x^2 + 6x + 8$ cups of flour. Convert this to vertex form.
A construction site has a parabolic arch with equation $y = x^2 - 2x - 3$. Convert this to vertex form.
The height of a projectile is given by $y = -x^2 + 4x + 2$. Complete the square to rewrite this equation.
A baker is making a cake with $x^2 + 8x + 12$ cups of sugar. Convert this to vertex form.
The cost of building a house is given by $y = x^2 + 10x + 5$. Complete the square to rewrite this equation.
A carpenter has a piece of wood with a parabolic shape given by $y = x^2 - 6x - 2$. Convert this to vertex form.
The volume of water in a tank is given by $y = x^2 + 2x - 3$. Complete the square to rewrite this equation.
A construction company is building an arch for a new bridge. The equation of the arch is $y = x^2 - 8x + 7$. Convert this equation to vertex form and interpret the results.
A baker is making a cake with a parabolic shape given by $y = x^2 + 4x + 2$. Complete the square to rewrite this equation and explain the significance of the vertex in this context.

teacher_rules: Ensure students understand the concept of completing the square
Provide examples of real-world applications of quadratic equations
Encourage students to interpret the results of completing the square in different contexts
